dev_plans.content: Lightweight heuristic approach using pure NumPy/scikit-learn. At the end of each training epoch, run the model on the validation set and collect predicted probabilities, predicted labels, true labels, and the ethnicity subgroup code (0-5 for the 6 groups) for every sample. Group the predictions by ethnicity using a pandas groupby on a DataFrame of (y_true, y_prob, y_pred, ethnicity). For each subgroup compute: AUROC via sklearn.metrics.roc_auc_score, AUPRC via average_precision_score, sensitivity (TP/(TP+FN)), specificity (TN/(TN+FP)), and Brier score (mean((y_prob - y_true)**2)). Store all five metrics per group in a dict-of-dicts. Compute the overall-population version of each metric for reference. Define the primary disparity signal as AUROC_gap = max_group_AUROC - min_group_AUROC, and log it as a scalar to TensorBoard/W&B along with the per-group AUROCs. Also log AUPRC_gap, sensitivity_gap, specificity_gap, Brier_gap, and the standard deviation of AUROC across groups. Insert the probe as a validation hook called at the end of validation_epoch_end (Lightning) or inside the epoch loop after evaluate() (plain PyTorch). Produce two artifacts per epoch: (1) a single scalar AUROC_gap pushed to the logger so a time-series chart is automatically drawn; (2) a CSV row appended to fairness_log.csv containing epoch, per-group metrics, and all five gaps. At end of training write one summary PNG via matplotlib with one subplot per metric showing the time-series of gaps with the threshold line drawn horizontally. No external fairness library needed, ~80 lines of code.
dev_plans.metric: AUROC gap across the 6 ethnicity subgroups per epoch (max group AUROC minus min group AUROC on the validation set)
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — based on the probe's own unhealthy criterion (>5% AUROC gap) and the Chen et al. 2021 / Röösli et al. 2022 convention that clinically actionable subgroup disparity begins around 5 AUROC points; gaps under 0.05 are generally reported as within-noise for MIMIC-scale validation sets.

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous approach with bootstrap confidence intervals and equalized-odds testing. During validation at every epoch, collect (y_true, y_prob, ethnicity) tuples for the full validation set. For each of the 6 subgroups, compute AUROC/AUPRC/sensitivity/specificity/Brier plus 95% bootstrap CIs by resampling the subgroup indices with replacement B=1000 times (use numpy.random.Generator for speed, vectorize roc_auc_score per resample, or use scikit-learn's utils.resample). Compute equalized odds difference as EOD = max_g |TPR_g - TPR_overall| + max_g |FPR_g - FPR_overall|, and demographic parity difference DPD = max_g P(ŷ=1|g) - min_g P(ŷ=1|g). Use a studentized bootstrap or DeLong's test (via the `delong` helper or `pROC` equivalent ported to Python) to produce a p-value for pairwise AUROC differences between the largest subgroup and each minority subgroup; apply Bonferroni correction across the 5 pairwise tests. Emit a structured record per epoch with point estimates, CI lower/upper, and corrected p-values. The primary scalar logged is AUROC_gap with CI; secondary scalars EOD, DPD, and number_of_significant_minority_disparities (count of groups with corrected p<0.05 AND AUROC deficit >0.03). Insert the probe as a post-validation callback; cache the bootstrap indices across epochs to make trajectories comparable. Produce: (1) per-epoch JSONL log; (2) a forest plot PNG at each checkpoint showing per-group AUROC with CI whiskers; (3) a time-series plot of AUROC_gap with the 0.05 horizontal threshold line and a shaded band for the bootstrap CI of the gap itself. Use ~300 lines; runtime overhead dominated by bootstrap (parallelize with joblib).
dev_plans.metric: Bootstrap-estimated AUROC gap across the 6 ethnicity subgroups per epoch (point estimate of max-min AUROC, with 95% CI logged as a secondary band)
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — matches the probe's unhealthy-signal definition and the Pfohl et al. 2021 JBI guideline that a statistically significant AUROC deficit of ≥5 points in a protected subgroup is the operational flagging criterion; with B=1000 bootstrap samples on validation sets >1k, a gap below 0.05 typically has CI overlapping zero.

dev_plans.content: Tooling-based approach using Fairlearn plus AIF360 for standardized fairness metrics. Add fairlearn>=0.10 and aif360>=0.6 to requirements. Wrap the model's validation step to yield a BinaryLabelDataset (AIF360) and a fairlearn MetricFrame. After each epoch: (1) construct a MetricFrame with metrics={'auroc': roc_auc_score, 'auprc': average_precision_score, 'sensitivity': true_positive_rate, 'specificity': true_negative_rate, 'brier': brier_score_loss}, sensitive_features=ethnicity_series. MetricFrame.by_group gives per-group values; MetricFrame.difference(method='between_groups') gives the max-min gap for each metric in one call. (2) Use fairlearn.metrics.equalized_odds_difference and demographic_parity_difference directly on y_true, y_pred, sensitive_features. (3) Use AIF360's ClassificationMetric.disparate_impact and .average_odds_difference for cross-validation with the AIF360 literature. Log all scalars to MLflow/W&B under a 'fairness/' namespace: fairness/auroc_gap, fairness/auprc_gap, fairness/eod, fairness/dpd, fairness/disparate_impact, plus fairness/auroc_group_<k> for k in {White, Black, Hispanic, Asian, Native, Other}. Insert as a LightningModule on_validation_epoch_end hook or as a HuggingFace TrainerCallback.on_evaluate. Emit artifacts: (a) MetricFrame.to_html() dumped as mlflow artifact each epoch; (b) fairlearn's built-in grouped bar chart saved via MetricFrame.by_group.plot.bar(...) each epoch; (c) the auroc_gap time series auto-rendered by the experiment tracker, with a hard-coded horizontal threshold annotation added by a small utility (mlflow.log_figure after plotting the gap vs epoch with a matplotlib hlines at the threshold). Minimal glue code (~120 lines), with the benefit that future audits can re-open the MetricFrames and the AIF360 objects natively.
dev_plans.metric: fairlearn MetricFrame AUROC between-groups difference on validation set per epoch (equivalent to max-min AUROC across the 6 ethnicity subgroups)
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — aligned with the probe design's >5% flagging threshold and with Fairlearn/AIF360 documentation examples that use 0.05 as the reporting cutoff for an actionable performance disparity; also matches the four-fifths-rule-adjacent 0.8 disparate-impact ratio in AUROC-space for typical clinical prevalence.
